en_source: Whatever today holds, you can be here for a moment.
I am having a hard moment
A calm route to real human support, a trusted contact or a region-correct line, will live here.
You deserve real support. These are calm ways to reach a person.
If you need someone right now
We don't have verified local services for this region yet. These trusted options can help you find support near you.
So Aspen can fit you a little better. There are no right answers, nothing here is a test or a diagnosis, and you can skip anything. Take your time.
What would feel most helpful from Aspen right now?
A calmer moment in hard times
Somewhere private to put my thoughts
Company that doesn't ask much of me
A way to reach real help
How would you describe your relationship with eating lately?
Out of my control at times
Something I try to undo afterwards
Hard because of how foods feel, taste, or seem
Mostly about how I feel in my body
Do you find yourself holding back from eating, even when part of you wants or needs to?
Do you ever feel that once you start eating, it's hard to feel in control?
After eating, do you often feel a strong urge to make up for it somehow?
Is eating hard mainly because of how foods feel, taste, or smell, or worry about what eating might do, rather than worry about your body or shape?
How much do thoughts about your body or shape affect your day?
How much is this getting in the way of your everyday life right now?
Do you have anyone, a professional or someone you trust, supporting you with this?
On the hardest days, would it help to have a quick way to reach a person?

Aspen will gently shape itself around what you've said, and you can change any of this anytime. One thing worth saying clearly: this isn't a diagnosis or an assessment. If you'd like to find real, specialist support, here's where to start.
Here are a few quiet things that can help. There's no wrong choice.
Follow the words at your own pace.
Slowly, gently, take each one in. There's no rush.
Urges rise, crest, and pass, like a wave. You don't have to act on it. Stay here a moment and let it move through.
A private place for your thoughts. Only you can see this.
Nothing here yet, and that's completely okay. This space is here whenever you'd like it.
A few words, if you'd like…
If it helps, what are you feeling?
Things change. You can answer again anytime.
Permanently removes your reflections and notes from this device.
This permanently removes everything you've written on this device. It can't be undone.
Want some company for a bit?
Want to sit together for a minute?
I'm not going anywhere. We can just be here.
Rough moment? I can get you to your calm tools, or to someone.
This feeling is like weather. It moves. I'll stay while it does.
Want to breathe with me for a minute?
Let's notice the room together, slowly.
Want to put it into words? I'll hold onto them.
Just here if you want company.
Your quiet tools are right here if today needs them.
Off. Your words never leave this device.
On. You can turn this off anytime.
Turning this on means what you write in the reflection space is sent securely to an AI service to give better responses. You can turn it off anytime, and everything it holds can be deleted.
Everything else in Aspen works fully on this device, with no AI service involved.
Developer-only view of the companion voice and safety guard.
If you want, tell me how it felt…
Not available right now. Your notebook still works, always.
That sounds like a lot to carry. You deserve real support right now, from a real person.
I can't respond the way I'd want to here. What you're feeling matters, and a real person can be there for it.
Text to run through the guard…
Off. A tiny pixel friend can sit quietly in the corner, only if you want.
Keeping you company. Tap to let it rest.
Your companion. Tap to play together.
The companion can sit at the edge of your screen, outside Aspen too.

Android will ask you to allow Aspen to “display over other apps”. That only lets the companion sit on your screen — Aspen cannot see, read, or record anything you do there. It hides on its own during videos and games, and you can let it rest anytime.
Once in a while, a gentle hello. Nothing is ever asked of you.
A quiet hello from your companion, once in a while.
Everything works without one. An account only adds the choice to back up later.
Signed in. Your writing still lives on this device.
Your writing stays on this device either way.
Account ID (your way back in if no email is attached): %1$s
That didn't match. Take your time and try again whenever.
That email already has an account. You can sign in instead.
A longer password is needed here.
Aspen couldn't reach the server just now. Everything on this device is unaffected.
Ends the session on this device. Your writing stays.
Removes the account and anything backed up. Writing on this device stays.
The account and anything backed up to it will be permanently removed. Everything written on this device stays here, untouched.
Keep an encrypted copy with your account. It's locked before it leaves your phone.
On. Backed up only when you choose.
Your backup is locked with this passphrase before it leaves your phone. We store only what we cannot read, so we can never unlock it for you.
A longer passphrase is needed here.
Aspen couldn't reach the server just now. Nothing has changed.

If the passphrase is ever forgotten, this code is the only other way into your backup. Keep it somewhere safe outside this phone. We cannot recover it for you — an email reset brings back your sign-in, never your backup.
Replaces your last backup with what's written here now.
Restored. Your writing from the backup is here now.
That didn't open the backup. The passphrase or the recovery code — either works here.
There's no backup on this account yet.
Removes the backup from the server. Writing on this device stays.
The encrypted copy on the server will be deleted. Everything on this device stays as it is.
Keeps the little companion on your screen. Quiet by design.
It sits quietly at the edge of the screen. You can let it rest anytime from Aspen.
